edges.description: Severe hypothyroidism drives multisystem respiratory and cardiovascular decompensation in myxedema coma.
Thyroid hormone dysregulation affects skin homeostasis and contributes to cutaneous myxedema manifestations.
intermediate_mechanisms: Thyroid hormone effects on skin homeostasis

edges.description: Low cardiac output and hypovolemia stimulate ADH release, impairing free water excretion.
Respiratory decompensation can progress through hypercarbia and CNS depression to coma.
Cardiovascular depression in myxedema coma includes bradycardia.
Cardiovascular involvement in myxedema coma includes pericardial fluid accumulation.
Reduced ventilatory drive produces hypoventilation with hypercarbia.
In-hospital hypotension is part of the severe decompensated presentation and predicts mortality.
ADH-mediated water retention contributes directly to low sodium in myxedema coma.
Cutaneous myxedema produces non-pitting edema of the face, hands, and feet.
Cutaneous involvement includes dry, scaly skin.
Tissue edema in myxedema can include macroglossia.
Hair thinning or sparse body hair is part of the skin and appendage involvement in myxedema coma.

nodes.description: Decreased cardiac output and hypovolemia can stimulate ADH release, reducing free water excretion and contributing to hyponatremia in myxedema coma.

evidence.snippet: Decreased cardiac output and hypovolemia sensed by baroreceptors may lead to a stimulation of antidiuretic hormone (ADH) release, further contributing to hyponatremia and impaired free water excretion.
Findings in the cardiovascular system include pericardial effusion, cardiomegaly, bradycardia, and reduced ejection fraction and cardiac output due to decreased cardiac contractility.
the most dramatic aspect of the presentation is the coma per se which typically evolves from initial lethargy, then progressing to a history of increased sleeping throughout the day

nodes.description: Severe thyroid hormone dysregulation affects skin homeostasis and can produce diffuse non-pitting edema and thickened skin. The cutaneous mechanism is modeled as abnormal aminoglycan and hyaluronan metabolism in the skin.

On physical examination there will be dry, scaly skin, nonpitting edema of the faces, hands, and feet, macroglossia, delayed deep tendon reflexes, and thinning or sparse body hair.

Other laboratory test abnormalities can include low blood glucose, low sodium and chloride levels, high total and ionized calcium, and mild renal failure with elevations in blood urea nitrogen (BUN) and creatinine.

evidence.reference_title: Utility of myxedema score as a predictor of mortality in myxedema coma.
evidence.snippet: in-hospital hypotension (OR 9.1, p value 0.020), and high qSOFA score (OR 7.1, p value 0.023) predicted mortality.
Even in the absence of pulmonary infection, there will be hypoxemia and hypercarbia secondary to a reduced ventilatory drive triggered by hypercapnia that forms the basis for respiratory depression.

nodes.description: Profound hypothyroidism can reduce ventilatory drive and cardiac output, contributing to hypoventilation, hypoxemia or hypercarbia, bradycardia, hypotension, serous effusions, and coma.

evidence.snippet: via a common pathway of respiratory decompensation with carbon dioxide narcosis
Findings in the cardiovascular system include pericardial effusion, cardiomegaly, bradycardia, and reduced ejection fraction and cardiac output due to decreased cardiac contractility.

nodes.description: Myxedema is the severe end of hypothyroidism, with reduced thyroid hormone signaling causing systemic slowing of metabolism and, in decompensated cases, altered mental status, hypothermia, and multiple organ-system abnormalities.
